description: Phase 20-21: Analytics & Business Intelligence + Launch Readiness. Extends analytics package with predictive analytics, funnel optimization, and attribution modeling. Extends observability package with SLO/SLA enforcement, chaos engineering recipes, and launch readiness tooling. Adds load testing configurations and deployment strategies.
steps: 1. In packages/analytics/src/core/predictive-analytics.ts: Implement PredictiveAnalytics class with time-series forecasting using triple exponential smoothing (Holt-Winters with additive/multiplicative seasonality), trend decomposition into seasonal + trend + residual components, confidence interval calculation using prediction error variance, anomaly detection on business metrics using forecast deviation, multi-step ahead forecasting with increasing uncertainty bands, what-if scenario modeling with parameter perturbation

2. In packages/analytics/src/core/attribution-model.ts: Implement AttributionModel class with multi-touch attribution models: last-touch, first-touch, linear (equal credit), time-decay (exponential weight toward conversion), position-based (40/20/40), data-driven (Shapley value approximation using marginal contribution), channel interaction effects, attribution window management, cross-device journey stitching using probabilistic matching

3. In packages/analytics/src/core/funnel-optimizer.ts: Implement FunnelOptimizer class with multi-step funnel definition and tracking, drop-off rate calculation per step with statistical significance, bottleneck identification using conversion rate variance, segment comparison (comparing funnels across user segments), funnel velocity (time between steps with percentiles), predictive completion probability using historical completion rates, optimal funnel path discovery using Markov chain transitions

4. In packages/analytics/src/core/user-segmentation.ts: Implement UserSegmentation class with RFM analysis (Recency-Frequency-Monetary scoring with quintile bucketing), behavioral clustering using k-means with Euclidean distance, dynamic segment membership with real-time updates, segment overlap analysis, lifecycle stage classification, lookalike audience generation using feature similarity, segment growth/shrink rate tracking

5. In packages/analytics/src/core/event-pipeline.ts: Implement EventPipeline class with high-throughput event ingestion with batching, event schema validation and enrichment, sessionization using timeout-based session boundaries (30min inactivity), event deduplication using idempotency keys, late-arriving event handling with watermarks, event replay from persistent store, sampling strategies for high-volume events (reservoir sampling)
6. Create packages/launch-readiness/ with standard structure: package.json (@quant/launch-readiness), tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, src/types.ts, src/core/

7. In packages/launch-readiness/src/types.ts: Define types for SLODefinition, SLAContract, ErrorBudget, ChaosExperiment, LoadTestConfig, DeploymentStrategy, HealthCheck, Runbook, IncidentSeverity, CapacityPlan, FeatureFlagConfig, CanaryMetrics, RollbackTrigger, BlueGreenState

8. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/slo-engine.ts: Implement SLOEngine class with SLO definition (target %, measurement window, burn rate thresholds), error budget calculation (1 - target) * total_requests in window, burn rate alerting (1x = consuming budget at normal rate, 2x = double rate, 14.4x = will exhaust in 1 hour), multi-window burn rate alerts (short window catches spikes, long window confirms trends), SLO vs SLA relationship tracking, error budget policy enforcement (freeze deploys when budget < 10%)

9. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/chaos-recipes.ts: Implement ChaosRecipes class with chaos experiment catalog: latency injection (add configurable delay to % of requests), error injection (return 500s at configured rate), resource exhaustion simulation (memory pressure, CPU saturation), dependency failure simulation (circuit breaker triggering), network partition simulation (drop % of cross-service traffic), experiment scheduling with safety guards (auto-abort if error rate exceeds threshold), blast radius control (limit to % of traffic, specific regions)

10. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/load-test-runner.ts: Implement LoadTestRunner class with workload modeling: constant rate, ramp-up (linear increase over time), spike (sudden jump), stress (find breaking point), soak (extended duration), request distribution using percentile-based latency targets, virtual user simulation with think time, throughput measurement with Little's Law verification (L = lambda * W), saturation point detection using utilization vs response time curve, result analysis with statistical summaries (min/max/mean/p50/p95/p99/p999)

11. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/deployment-strategies.ts: Implement DeploymentStrategies class with blue-green deployment orchestration (traffic switch with instant rollback), canary deployment with progressive traffic shifting (1% -> 5% -> 25% -> 50% -> 100%), canary metrics comparison using statistical hypothesis testing (chi-squared test for error rates), automatic rollback triggers (error rate, latency, saturation), feature flag integration for dark launches, deployment pipeline stage gates with approval workflows

12. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/capacity-planner.ts: Implement CapacityPlanner class with traffic forecasting using linear regression on historical data, resource requirement estimation (CPU/memory/network per request), autoscaling configuration generator (target utilization, min/max instances), cost modeling per deployment configuration, headroom calculation for traffic spikes (2x burst, 10x viral event), database connection pool sizing using queuing theory (M/M/c model approximation)

13. In packages/launch-readiness/src/core/runbook-engine.ts: Implement RunbookEngine class with structured runbook definition (trigger condition, steps, verification, rollback), automated step execution where possible, escalation chain with timeout-based promotion, incident severity classification (P1-P5 with response time SLAs), post-incident review template generation, runbook coverage tracking (which alerts have runbooks)
14. Update packages/analytics/src/types.ts with new types for predictive analytics, attribution, funnels, segmentation, event pipeline
15. Update barrel exports for analytics and launch-readiness packages
acceptance_criteria: packages/analytics/src/core/ has 5 new files implementing real analytics algorithms (Holt-Winters, Shapley values, k-means, RFM, Markov chains)
packages/launch-readiness/ exists with 6 core files implementing real operations algorithms (burn rates, Little's Law, chi-squared tests, M/M/c queuing)
All implementations use proper statistical/mathematical formulas, not placeholder logic
Type definitions are comprehensive and properly used throughout implementations
Running pnpm turbo typecheck passes for all modified packages
